idea.hook: Andromeda's collision is millions of years away... but how long would it take to *visit*?
facts: Andromeda Galaxy is 2.537 million light-years (ly) away.
Traveling at light speed (c), the journey would take 2.537 million years.
Our fastest spacecraft, Parker Solar Probe (692,000 km/h), would take 3.96 BILLION years!
idea.payoff: Intergalactic travel is a cosmic time capsule – even at light speed, the journey is longer than human civilization. The universe truly is immense!
idea.title: Andromeda: How Fast Can We Go? 💨🌌

scenes.text:
Andromeda: Millions of years away...
But how long to VISIT?
scenes.visual: Starts with a wide view of Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies side-by-side, slowly zooming towards Andromeda. Text appears over this.
Andromeda galaxy centered, with a small Earth icon appearing on the left and an arrow/line stretching to Andromeda, highlighting the distance number.
Background of streaking stars, simulating extreme speed. A simple line graphic of a light beam quickly traversing, then the text appears.
A stylized icon or simple rendering of the Parker Solar Probe flying across the screen, followed by the enormous number appearing with emphasis.

scenes.text:
Intergalactic travel:
A cosmic time capsule.
The Universe is IMMENSE!
scenes.visual: Slowly zooming out from Andromeda, revealing more and more galaxies in an increasingly vast cosmic web. The text appears gradually, with 'IMMENSE!' pulsing slightly.

youtube.title: Andromeda: How Fast Can We Go? 💨🌌
